behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed (not only direct children of the root).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real OS filesystem watching behavior (recursive/child-path event delivery), not just in-process logic. MOCK-HIDE applies: a fake watcher can emit idealized recursive events and miss real platform watcher gaps. Rejected higher levels: behavior is purely filesystem/event handling with no AI/ML judgment and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temporary directory tree. Create `watched/subdir/new.txt` on disk and verify one transformed output is written to the output directory. Assert it is processed without daemon restart and that direct-root-only filtering is not required.

behaviors.description: Deduplication discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior text targets dedup policy logic itself (same key + TTL window), which can fail inside one object/function boundary without I/O. Rejected higher levels with disqualifying text: "deduplication logic correctly discards" and "same file path within a configurable time window" do not require OS events, subprocesses, network, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a fake/controlled clock. Submit request for `/x/a.txt`, then resubmit same path before window expiry and assert second is rejected; advance time past window and assert request is accepted again.

behaviors.description: When the OS emits multiple filesystem events for one file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real OS event duplication/coalescing semantics. Exact failure appears at watcher/OS boundary; in-process event fakes can miss real duplicate patterns/timing. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real watched directory. Write a file using an operation known to trigger multiple events on the target OS (e.g., temp-write + rename or append sequence). Verify only one pipeline execution/output for that file path/content.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and recreated while running, daemon resumes watching the new directory without restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS/filesystem lifecycle behavior (inode/path replacement, watcher invalidation, reattach). Real failure occurs only with actual directory deletion/recreation events. MOCK-HIDE applies because a stub watcher can unrealistically preserve subscriptions. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment or agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon watching a real directory. Delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file. Assert daemon remains running and processes the post-recreate file without manual restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, processing follows creation-timestamp order.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on real filesystem timestamp behavior under concurrent arrivals (resolution, stat timing, event race). In-process fakes can hide platform-specific ordering defects. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-step agent loop.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times (or tiny stagger where needed for timestamp resolution). Capture processing order and assert it matches creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file still being written when arrival fires is processed only after write completion.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/locking/flush boundary: event may fire before durable completion, and daemon must avoid premature pickup. Fakes usually deliver fully-written fixtures and hide partial-write races. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision-making or agentic workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watched/output directories. Begin a slow/chunked write to a large file (keep descriptor open), trigger arrival event, and verify daemon does not process mid-write. Close/finish write, then assert exactly one successful processing occurs after completion.
